data.title: Brian Duyck recognized for 50+ years of expertise in farming and irrigation by Inner Circle as Pinnacle Professional Member.
data.description: In Powell, Wyoming on February 16, 2026, Brian Duyck was acknowledged in The Inner Circle as a distinguished Pinnacle Professional Member of the Inner Circle of Excellence for his noteworthy achievements in Agriculture. Brian Duyck, boasting over five decades of expertise in farming and irrigation, was lauded for his significant contributions to the field.

data.title: HR consultant Nicole E. Miller recognized by Inner Circle as Pinnacle Professional Member for advancing people-first leadership amid rising workplace burnout.
data.description: Renowned HR expert Nicole E. Miller has been commended for promoting a people-centric leadership approach during a time when workplace burnout is on the rise. Hailing from Hudson, Florida, and gaining recognition on February 16, 2026, Nicole's efforts come at a critical time when many businesses are grappling with significant challenges. These include mounting cases of leadership fatigue, decreased employee involvement, and growing uncertainty within the workforce.

data.title: Aaron Dickens recognized as Inner Circle Lifetime member for contributions to consulting, technology innovation, and leadership.
data.description: On February 16th, 2026 in Marietta, Georgia, Aaron Dickens was highlighted in The Inner Circle and honored as an Inner Circle Lifetime member for his achievements in consulting, technology innovation, and leadership development. He has displayed exemplary contributions in these areas. Aaron's recognition emphasizes his significant impact on the industry.

data.title: William P Moon Jr. recognized as Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence for his real estate contributions.
data.description: In Oil City, Pennsylvania, on February 16, 2026, William P. Moon Jr. was acknowledged in The Inner Circle as a distinguished Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence due to his notable accomplishments in Residential and Commercial Real Estate. With a career spanning nearly two decades, William P. Moon Jr. has established himself as a leading figure in the industry.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum hold steady at $67,858.35 and $1,967.49 respectively. Dogecoin slips to $0.1004. 93,334 traders liquidated for $249.32M in 24 hours. Spot Bitcoin ETFs see $15.2M inflows, Ethereum ETFs see $10.3M. XRP gains strength against Bitcoin as liquidity expands.
data.description: Bitcoin is currently sitting close to the $68,000 mark, without any clear driving force behind the broader cryptocurrency market. The latest cryptocurrency prices are as follows: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) at $67,858.35, Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) at $1,967.49, Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) at $84.54, XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) at $1.48, Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) at $0.1004, and Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) at $0.056496. Some notable statistics reveal that within the past 24 hours, 93,334 traders faced liquidations amounting to $249.32 million. Additionally, there were net inflows of $15.2 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs and $10.3 million from spot Ethereum ETFs on Friday, according to SoSoValue data. In terms of the top performers in the last day, Stable, MemeCore, and Nexo were among the gainers. An interesting development is the strengthening of XRP against Bitcoin as liquidity expands. For further details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Partners at Alpharetta estate planning firm rank in top 5% of Georgia lawyers, named to 2026 Georgia Super Lawyers list.
data.description: Alpharetta, GA - On February 16, 2026, Morgan and DiSalvo, P.C., a renowned estate planning firm, revealed that both partners secured positions on the 2026 Georgia Super Lawyers list. This reaffirms their standing in the legal community as leading professionals in their field.

data.title: Oilmar DMCC celebrates 10 years by launching "10 Years, 10 Charities, One Responsibility" initiative in Dubai, UAE.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE, Oilmar DMCC celebrated its 10-year milestone and introduced "10 Years. 10 Charities. One Responsibility." initiative. This program ties each year of the company's existence to charitable organizations and causes that resonate with its principles.

data.title: SwRI's 78th annual meeting in San Antonio highlighted advances in robotics, space tech, and energy with a focus on safety.
data.description: In San Antonio, Southwest Research Institute hosted its 78th Annual Meeting where advisory trustees, board of directors, and guests convened at SwRI's headquarters. The institute's staff discussed progress in robotics for maritime operations and space exploration, along with introducing the Machinery Innovation Center for High-Energy Fluids. Additionally, SwRI engineers are tackling infrastructure issues and energy requirements for data centers. The meeting highlighted these developments and emphasized the importance of safety measures.
